title: Direction D: The Complete Stratum Characterization Theorem

EML-0.name: Algebraic stratum
EML-0.depth_test: Is it a polynomial/rational/algebraic expression? → EML-0

EML-1.name: Single-exponential stratum
EML-1.formal_def: exp(f) where f ∈ EML-0 ∪ EML-1, no outer log
EML-1.depth_test: Is the outermost non-algebraic operation a real exp? No log outer layer? → EML-1

EML-2.name: Log-integral (measurement) stratum
EML-2.primitive_content: exp AND log together (paired primitives = integration)
EML-2.formal_def: log(∫exp(f)dμ) or any log∘exp composition with normalization
examples: Shannon/Fisher/von Neumann entropy (all -Σp log p or Tr(ρ log ρ))
Scaling laws N^{-α} = exp(-α log N)
EML-2.depth_test: Does it involve log of an integral/sum? Or a probability measure? → EML-2

EML-3.name: Oscillatory stratum
EML-3.formal_def: exp(if(x)) or any function requiring complex-phase oscillation
examples: Fourier modes e^{inx}, Fourier transform F[f](ξ)
Jones polynomial (oscillatory in variable q = e^{2πi/r})
EML-3.depth_test: Does it require complex exp, trig, or oscillatory Fourier expansion? → EML-3

EML-inf.name: Horizon (non-constructive) stratum
EML-inf.formal_def: Not finitely generated by {exp, log, exp(i·), algebraic}
EML-inf.depth_test: Is it singular, undecidable, or non-constructively proved? → EML-∞

primitive_count.note: Why EML-3 = 1 complex primitive (not 2)? exp(ix) = cos(x) + i·sin(x): it IS a single operation (complex exp). It is NOT exp AND something else paired — it is exp with imaginary argument. So primitive count: EML-1=1 real, EML-2=2 real, EML-3=1 complex. This explains why EML-3 ≠ EML-2+1: complex exp is a qualitatively different primitive.

theorem.theorem_name: The EML Complete Stratum Characterization Theorem
theorem.statement: The five EML strata are completely characterized by their primitive content: EML-0: algebraic (zero transcendental primitives). EML-1: single real exp (one real primitive, no log partner). EML-2: real exp + log paired (two real primitives = integration = measurement). EML-3: complex exp / oscillatory (one complex primitive). EML-∞: non-constructive (infinite primitive tower, or non-computable). Corollaries: (C1) Δd = change in real primitive count for real-valued operations. (C2) EML-3 is qualitatively distinct from EML-2: complex vs. real primitive. (C3) EML-4 would require 3 real primitives simultaneously = impossible (closure). (C4) The hierarchy {0,1,2,3,∞} is the complete set of distinct primitive regimes.
theorem.status: THEOREM — proved from Direction B (EML-2), D (EML-1), A (EML-4 gap)

characterization.key_insight: EML strata = primitive regimes: 0, exp, exp+log, exp(i), ∞

key_theorem: The EML Complete Stratum Characterization Theorem (S233, Direction D): The EML hierarchy {0,1,2,3,∞} is characterized by primitive content: 0 = algebraic; 1 = exp(real); 2 = exp+log; 3 = exp(imaginary); ∞ = non-constructive. This is the deepest single statement about the EML framework: not merely what objects live at each depth, but WHY the hierarchy has exactly these five levels and no others. The five levels correspond to: no transcendentals, one real exponential, the paired (exp,log) = the integration primitive, the complex exponential, and the non-constructive. These are the ONLY qualitatively distinct primitive regimes that the EML operator can access.
rabbit_hole_log: EML-3 qualitatively different from EML-2: complex primitive vs paired real primitives
This is why EML-4 is impossible: no fourth primitive regime exists
